lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸੁ ਕਸੁ ਟਟਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਤਪੈ ਤੈ ਵਿਲਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ras kas ttattar paaeeai tapai tai vilalaae |
lines.english: What punishment is inflicted upon it! Its juice is extracted and placed in the cauldron; as it is heated, it groans and cries out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੁੰਢਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਰਖਿ ਕੈ ਦੇਨਿ ਸੁ ਮਲ ਸਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: khundtaa andar rakh kai den su mal sajaae |
lines.english: and then, it is placed between the wooden rollers and crushed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮਿਠੈ ਪਤਰੀਐ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਲੋਕਾ ਆਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak mitthai patareeai vekhahu lokaa aae |2|
lines.english: Nanak: come, people, and see how the sweet sugar-cane is treated! ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਸੋ ਫੋਗੁ ਸਮਾਲੀਐ ਦਿਚੈ ਅਗਿ ਜਾਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bhee so fog samaaleeai dichai ag jaalaae |
lines.english: And then, the crushed cane is collected and burnt in the fire below.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਮਰਣੁ ਨ ਚਿਤਿ ਆਸ ਘਣੇਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa maran na chit aas ghaneriaa |
lines.english: Some do not think of death; they entertain great hopes.
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਿ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮਹਿ ਨਿਤ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਕੇਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: mar mar jameh nit kisai na keriaa |
lines.english: They die, and are re-born, and die, over and over again. They are of no use at all!
The King of the Angels of Death hunts down those self-willed manmukhs, over and over again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਲੂਣ ਹਾਰਾਮ ਕਿਆ ਨ ਜਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh loon haaraam kiaa na jaaniaa |
lines.english: The manmukhs are false to their own selves; they feel no gratitude for what they have been given.
In their conscious minds, they call themselves good.
They worship the Lord and bow at His Throne. They fulfill their pre-ordained destiny. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਭਾਵਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: sach milai mukh naam saahib bhaavasee |
lines.english: Those who attain the True Lord and chant His Name are pleasing to the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਧੇ ਕਰਨਿ ਸਲਾਮ ਖਸਮ ਨ ਭਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: badhe karan salaam khasam na bhaaniaa |
lines.english: Those who merely perform rituals of worship are not pleasing to their Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਥਰ ਪਾਲਾ ਕਿਆ ਕਰੇ ਖੁਸਰੇ ਕਿਆ ਘਰ ਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: pathar paalaa kiaa kare khusare kiaa ghar vaas |
lines.english: What can cold do to a stone? What is married life to a eunuch?
First Mehl, Salok:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਛੀ ਤਾਰੂ ਕਿਆ ਕਰੇ ਪੰਖੀ ਕਿਆ ਆਕਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: machhee taaroo kiaa kare pankhee kiaa aakaas |
lines.english: What can deep water do to a fish? What can the vast sky do to a bird?

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੋਲਾ ਜੇ ਸਮਝਾਈਐ ਪੜੀਅਹਿ ਸਿੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਪਾਠ ॥
lines.translit: bolaa je samajhaaeeai parreeeh sinmrit paatth |
lines.english: You may try to teach a deaf person by reading the Simritees to him, but how will he learn?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਤੇ ਚੰਦਨੁ ਲਾਈਐ ਭੀ ਸੋ ਕੁਤੀ ਧਾਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: kute chandan laaeeai bhee so kutee dhaat |
lines.english: You may apply sandalwood oil to a dog, but he will still be a dog.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਣੇ ਸੁਇਨਾ ਪਾਈਐ ਚੁਣਿ ਚੁਣਿ ਖਾਵੈ ਘਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: chaune sueinaa paaeeai chun chun khaavai ghaas |
lines.english: You may place gold before a herd of cattle, but they will pick out the grass to eat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧਾ ਚਾਨਣਿ ਰਖੀਐ ਦੀਵੇ ਬਲਹਿ ਪਚਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: andhaa chaanan rakheeai deeve baleh pachaas |
lines.english: You may place a light before a blind man and burn fifty lamps, but how will he see?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੂਰਖ ਏਹਿ ਗੁਣ ਬੋਲੇ ਸਦਾ ਵਿਣਾਸੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak moorakh ehi gun bole sadaa vinaas |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, this is the nature of a fool-everything he speaks is useless and wasted. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਹਾ ਮਾਰਣਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਢਹੈ ਨ ਹੋਇ ਕਪਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: lohaa maaran paaeeai dtahai na hoe kapaas |
lines.english: You may add flux to iron and melt it, but it will not become soft like cotton.
First Mehl:
When pieces of bronze or gold or iron break,
their children may bring them back together in the world, and the bond is established.
the metal-smith welds them together again in the fire, and the bond is established.
If a husband leaves his wife,
There is a bond between love and words of sweetness.
When the king makes a demand, and it is met, the bond is established.
When the hungry man eats, he is satisfied, and the bond is established.
In the famine, the rain fills the streams to overflowing, and the bond is established.
Through goodness and truth, the dead establish a bond with the living.
The fool establishes his bonds only when he is slapped in the face.
When one speaks the Truth, a bond is established with the Holy Scriptures.
Such are the bonds which prevail in the world.
Nanak says this after deep reflection:
through the Lord's Praise, we establish a bond with His Court. ||2||
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸਾਜਿ ਕੈ ਆਪੇ ਕਰੇ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aape kudarat saaj kai aape kare beechaar |
lines.english: He Himself created and adorned the Universe, and He Himself contemplates it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਰੇ ਖਜਾਨੈ ਪਾਈਅਹਿ ਖੋਟੇ ਸਟੀਅਹਿ ਬਾਹਰ ਵਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: khare khajaanai paaeeeh khotte satteeeh baahar vaar |
lines.english: The genuine are placed in His Treasury, while the counterfeit are thrown away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਖੋਟੇ ਇਕਿ ਖਰੇ ਆਪੇ ਪਰਖਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: eik khotte ik khare aape parakhanahaar |
lines.english: Some are counterfeit, and some are genuine. He Himself is the Appraiser.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਟੇ ਸਚੀ ਦਰਗਹ ਸੁਟੀਅਹਿ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਗੈ ਕਰਹਿ ਪੁਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: khotte sachee daragah sutteeeh kis aagai kareh pukaar |
lines.english: The counterfeit are thrown out of the True Court-unto whom should they complain?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪਿਛੈ ਭਜਿ ਪਵਹਿ ਏਹਾ ਕਰਣੀ ਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: satigur pichhai bhaj paveh ehaa karanee saar |
lines.english: They should worship and follow the True Guru-this is the lifestyle of excellence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੀ ਦਰਗਹ ਮੰਨੀਅਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: sachee daragah maneean gur kai prem piaar |
lines.english: Those who have enshrined love and affection for the Guru, are honored in the True Court.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਖੋਟਿਅਹੁ ਖਰੇ ਕਰੇ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਵਾਰਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: satigur khottiahu khare kare sabad savaaranahaar |
lines.english: The True Guru converts the counterfeit into genuine; through the Word of the Shabad, He embellishes and exalts us.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਣਤ ਤਿਨਾ ਦੀ ਕੋ ਕਿਆ ਕਰੇ ਜੋ ਆਪਿ ਬਖਸੇ ਕਰਤਾਰਿ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: ganat tinaa dee ko kiaa kare jo aap bakhase karataar |12|
lines.english: Who can estimate the value of those who have been forgiven by the Creator Lord Himself? ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਜੇਰ ਜਿਮੀ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਪੀਰਾ ਮਸਾਇਕਾ ਰਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: ham jer jimee duneea peeraa masaaeikaa raaeaa |
lines.english: All the spiritual teachers, their disciples and the rulers of the world shall be buried under the ground.
The emperors shall also pass away; God alone is Eternal.
Salok, First Mehl:
Who else is there?
Neither the angels, nor the demons, nor human beings,
Nor the Siddhas, nor the seekers shall remain on the earth.
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||1||
First Mehl:
